# `UDP S->C 0x03/0x25` — Reliable/PlayerInfo

**Transport:** UDP  
**Direction:** S->C  
**Identifier:** `0x03/0x25`  
**Status:** verified

## Structure

UDP S→C 0x03/0x25 — `LongPlayerInfo`. Server's authoritative
broadcast of a nearby player's appearance + faction + cash.
Body sizes 61-86 bytes (varies with character name length).
Verified 2026-05-10 against `LongPlayerInfo.java` source.

```
[0..1]    map_id LE16              zone/map ID (e.g. 0x0002)
[2..5]    player_id LE32           target player's character UID
[6]       0x00                     CONSTANT
[7]       0x08                     CONSTANT
[8]       0x09                     CONSTANT
[9]       0x6c                     CONSTANT
[10]      0x02                     CONSTANT
[11..12]  0x40 0x40                CONSTANT
[13]      0xc4                     CONSTANT
[14]      base_rank u8             player's level/rank cap
[15]      0x00                     reserved (CR placeholder)
[16]      faction u8               player's faction id
[17]      0x00                     reserved
[18..21]  ff ff ff ff              CONSTANT (placeholder for inv)
[22]      back_weapon_id u8        ID of weapon visible on back
                                    (0xff = none)
[23]      hand_weapon_id u8        ID of equipped weapon
                                    (0xff = none)
[24..25]  hair_model LE16
[26..27]  beard_model LE16
[28..31]  ff ff ff ff              CONSTANT
[32]      0x0f                     CONSTANT
[33..34]  head_model LE16
[35]      head_texture u8
[36]      torso_texture u8
[37]      leg_texture u8
[38..43]  6× 0x00                  CONSTANT
[44..45]  torso_model LE16
[46..47]  leg_model LE16
[48]      name_len u8              = strlen(name) + 1
[49..N]   name CString             ASCII + 0x00
[N+1]     0x00                     reserved
[N+2]     0x04                     `CashReceipt` sub-tag marker
[N+3..N+6] cash LE32                player's current cash
```

The terminal `0x04 [cash LE32]` is the same TLV pattern used
in `CashReceipt` for HUD wallet updates — making this
single packet a "spawn this player with full state" message.

Sample retail bytes (catalog inner_data, 32-byte preview):
```
02 00 6b cb 01 00 40 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 e4 3d 33 04 00 ff ff ff ff b7 04 ff 03 62 04 00 00 00
^map^player_id  ^?  ^?  ^?  ^?  ^?  ^?  ^?  ^? ^? ^? ^?  ^? ^? ^? ^?
   map=0x0002, player_id=0x0001cb6b
```

## Variants

Single packet form. Body length varies (61-86B) primarily due
to character name length. The constants in the middle
(0x08 0x09 0x6c 0x02 0x40 0x40 0xc4) are byte-stable across
all 41 retail samples.

## Observed contexts

Top marker `IN_WORLD` correlates with player spawn-in events.
Emitted whenever a new player enters the local zone or
becomes visible (proximity-based).

41 emissions / 17 captures (every capture sees this) — the
PvP-heavy `RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE` sees 8, the social
`RETAIL_LONG_PARTY_A/B` see 6/5 (matches multiple players in
proximity).

## Open questions

- The 7-byte CONSTANT block at [7..13] (`08 09 6c 02 40 40 c4`)
  is byte-stable across all retail samples — looks like a
  fixed protocol-version / packet-format magic. Hardcoded to
  identical values in `LongPlayerInfo.java`.
- Bytes [18..21] = `ff ff ff ff` and [28..31] = `ff ff ff ff`:
  inventory-slot or equipment placeholders. Probably future
  expansion fields the modern Evolution client doesn't use.
- The 6-byte zero block at [38..43]: unknown. Possibly
  reserved for additional appearance models (cape, hands,
  feet, accessories?).

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_udp.LongPlayerInfo` — emits
the verified body. Constructed with `(Player pl, PlayerCharacter
pc, int mapId)` triplet.

Wired from:
- Player-spawn broadcast paths (proximity-based emission when
  another player enters visibility range).
- Zone-entry broadcasts when the local player zones in.

The trailing `[0x04][cash LE32]` is intentional — it activates
the HUD CASH widget on the receiving client (per
`hud_pool_path_confirmed.md` and `cash_and_falldamage_subops.md`).

The catch-all subtag handling in `SubtagRouter` distinguishes
`0x03/0x1f/0x25/0x04` (in-game cash transaction) from
`0x03/0x25/...` (this packet) by outer-context.
